name: files-api-design
hook: Files browser API design follows GitHub API patterns
body:
The files API uses GitHub's repos.getContent and related endpoints directly, mirroring the existing pattern in other API routes like branches/route.ts and prs/files/route.ts. The auth pattern (requireKodyAuth, getRequestAuth, setGitHubContext) is consistent across all API routes.

Why: Ensures consistency with existing codebase patterns and proper rate-limit handling via the shared github-client.
How to apply: When adding new API routes that interact with GitHub, always use getOctokit(), setGitHubContext(), and clearGitHubContext() in a try/finally pattern.
why: Following existing patterns ensures proper auth, rate-limit handling, and makes the codebase maintainable

name: files-context-hooks
hook: React context for complex component state management
body:
The FilesContext provides centralized state management for the file browser, similar to how other parts of the dashboard use context providers (e.g., auth-context.tsx, useGoals hook pattern).

Why: Complex state with multiple interacting components (FileTree, FileViewer, FileEditor, FileSearch, FileDiffViewer) benefits from centralized state management.
How to apply: Use useCallback for state setters to maintain referential equality and prevent unnecessary re-renders.
why: Centralized state management prevents prop drilling and ensures components stay in sync
